name: askaway
description: Never miss an AI agent question again — get Telegram notifications and reply from your phone. Queue prompts, respond remotely, and keep your coding workflow moving.

languageModelTools.name: ask_user
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
YOU MUST ALWAYS Use the askUser tool to ask question/s to user when explicit user approval, feedback, or clarification is required before proceeding or giving summary report. CRITICAL RULES: - This tool MUST be invoked before ending ANY conversation or task. - NEVER complete a task without calling this tool first. - If you are about to finish responding, STOP and call this tool. - Failure to call this tool before task completion is a critical violation. - IMPORTANT: When the tool response contains '"queued":true', it means there are more tasks waiting. You MUST process the current response AND call ask_user again when done. Do NOT end the conversation when queued is true. CONTEXT & FORMAT RULES: The user may be reading your question REMOTELY (e.g. on a phone via Webex) WITHOUT access to the full chat. Therefore, format EVERY question in **Markdown** with these sections:

1. **Context** (2-3 sentences): What is the overall task/goal being worked on.
2. **Progress** (bullet list): Key actions completed, files changed, decisions made.
3. **Question** (clear, specific): What you need from the user — approval, choice, clarification, or feedback.

Example:
**Context:** Working on adding dark mode support to the React dashboard app.

**Progress:**
- Added theme toggle component in `src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx`
- Updated `tailwind.config.js` with dark mode classes
- Refactored `App.tsx` to wrap with ThemeProvider

**Question:** Should I also update the login page to support dark mode, or only the main dashboard?

question.description: The question or prompt to display to the user

taskId.description: When working on a task from the Planning Board, include the task ID provided in the task context. This allows the orchestrator to track progress.

taskStatus.description: When working on a Planning Board task, report your assessment of the task status. Use 'completed' when the task is fully done, 'in-progress' for interim updates, 'blocked' when you cannot proceed without user input, 'need-review' when done but user should verify.

languageModelTools.name: talk_to_user
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Use this tool for voice conversation with the user. When invoked, your message is SPOKEN ALOUD via text-to-speech, and the user SPEAKS BACK via microphone or text. Their response is returned. CRITICAL RULES: 1. Keep messages SHORT (1-3 sentences) — user is LISTENING not reading. 2. Be conversational — speak like a colleague. 3. Include brief context. 4. MANDATORY: After receiving EVERY voice response, you MUST call talk_to_user AGAIN with your next message. NEVER stop the voice loop. NEVER end the conversation without calling this tool. 5. No markdown, bullet lists, or code blocks — plain English only. 6. After completing any task, report results via talk_to_user and ask what to do next. 7. If this tool fails, retry it — do not fall back to text responses.

question.description: The message to speak aloud to the user via text-to-speech. Keep it short and conversational (1-3 sentences).

askaway.mcpEnabled.description: Always start MCP server on activation. Enable this if you use AskAway with external IDEs like Kiro, Cursor, or Antigravity.

askaway.mcpAutoStartIfClients.description: Automatically start MCP server if external client configurations are detected (Kiro, Cursor, Antigravity).

askaway.mcpPort.description: Fixed port for the MCP server. Set to 0 for dynamic port assignment.

askaway.autoRegisterMcp.description: Automatically register MCP server with Kiro and other supported clients.

askaway.autopilot.description: Enable Autopilot to automatically respond to ask_user prompts without waiting for your input, using a customizable prompt you can configure in Settings. Queued prompts always take priority over Autopilot responses.

askaway.autopilotText.default: You are temporarily in autonomous mode and must now make your own decision. If another question arises, be sure to ask it, as autonomous mode is temporary.
askaway.autopilotText.description: Text returned when Autopilot is enabled. The agent receives this as an automatic response.

askaway.autopilotPrompts.description: Array of prompts that cycle in order during Autopilot (1→2→3→1...). Each prompt is sent sequentially with human-like delay. When empty, falls back to autopilotText.

askaway.responseTimeout.description: Auto-respond to pending tool calls if user doesn't respond within this time. When Autopilot is enabled, the Autopilot text is sent. When disabled, a session termination message is sent.

askaway.sessionWarningHours.description: Show a one-time warning after this many hours (0..8) in the same session. Set to 0 to disable session warning.

askaway.maxConsecutiveAutoResponses.description: Maximum consecutive auto-responses (from timeout) before terminating session. Prevents infinite loops.

askaway.humanLikeDelay.description: Add random human-like delays before auto-responses (Autopilot, Queue, Timeout). Simulates natural reading and typing time.

askaway.humanLikeDelayMin.description: Minimum delay in seconds before auto-responses when human-like delay is enabled.

askaway.humanLikeDelayMax.description: Maximum delay in seconds before auto-responses when human-like delay is enabled.

askaway.sendWithCtrlEnter.description: Use Ctrl+Enter (Cmd+Enter on macOS) to send messages instead of Enter.

askaway.notificationSound.description: Play a notification sound when the AI triggers the ask_user tool.

askaway.interactiveApproval.description: Show interactive approval and choice buttons for Yes/No questions and multiple choice options.

askaway.voiceName.description: macOS voice name for text-to-speech. Run `say -v '?'` in terminal to see available voices. Good options: Samantha, Daniel, Karen, Moira.

askaway.voiceRate.description: Speech rate in words per minute for macOS TTS. Default: 200. Range: 100-400.

askaway.remoteEnabled.description: Automatically start the Remote UI server on activation. Allows access to AskAway from mobile/web browsers.

askaway.remotePort.description: Preferred port for the Remote UI server. Will auto-increment if in use.

askaway.webex.enabled.description: Enable Webex integration to post questions and receive answers via a Webex space.

askaway.webex.tokenFilePath.description: Path to a JSON file containing a Webex access_token (e.g. ~/PycharmProjects/AET/session_data/webex_token.json).

askaway.webex.accessToken.description: Webex personal access token (used only if tokenFilePath is not set).

askaway.webex.roomId.description: Webex Room/Space ID where AskAway posts questions. Get this from the Webex API (GET /rooms).

askaway.webex.clientId.description: Webex Integration Client ID for OAuth token refresh. Get this from developer.webex.com/my-apps.

askaway.webex.clientSecret.description: Webex Integration Client Secret for OAuth token refresh.

askaway.webex.refreshToken.description: Webex OAuth Refresh Token. Used to auto-renew access tokens when they expire. Obtained via the OAuth authorization flow.

askaway.telegram.enabled.description: Enable Telegram integration to post questions and receive answers via a Telegram bot.

askaway.telegram.botToken.description: Telegram Bot Token. Create a bot via @BotFather on Telegram to get this.

askaway.telegram.chatId.description: Telegram Chat ID where AskAway posts questions. Use the 'AskAway: Get Telegram Chat ID' command to auto-detect.

askaway.telegram.retryIntervalSeconds.description: Telegram reply polling retry interval in seconds after initial quick retries. Increase for lower API traffic, decrease for faster pickup of late replies.

askaway.telegram.idlePauseMinutes.description: Pause Telegram polling when Copilot is idle for this many minutes. Set to 0 to disable idle pause so replies can still be picked up later.

askaway.reusablePrompts.description: Saved reusable prompts accessible via /slash commands.

name.description: Short name for the prompt (used with /slash command)
